CREVERSE
Arrival & Onboarding Guide

Arrival & Onboarding Guide

for ONLINE training instructors going directly to branch-prepared housing upon arrival in Korea

Updated: 05.18.2026

Your Accommodation Route

You will go directly from Incheon Airport to your branch-prepared housing.

Timeline:
Arrival Day: Travel directly to the apartment prepared by your branch.

Week 1: Stay at your apartment and complete online training via Zoom.

Post-training: May have in-housing training and branch onboarding at your assigned branch. Branch staff will assist with the medical check, Alien Registration Card (ARC), and bank account setup.

Apartment information: Your branch-prepared housing details will be provided before your departure for Korea.
I just landed Show taxi card Housing move-in Contact help
Who to Contact When...
Quick Contact Guide

Use this guide to decide who to contact during arrival, online training, housing move-in, and branch onboarding.

SituationPerson in charge
Flight, receipt, or arrival questionRecruiter
Apartment address, housing move-in, branch directions, shadowing, in-housing training, onboarding, medical check, ARC, or bank account setupBranch Staff
Online training schedule, Zoom link, Training Information Packet, or training-related questionTraining Center / Training Team
Change to arrival plan or urgent arrival-period issueRecruiter / Placement Manager
Emergency situationEmergency Services / Foreigner Hotline
Reminder: Most communication with branch staff, managers, and training staff will happen through KakaoTalk, Zoom, or email. Set up KakaoTalk before departure and check KakaoTalk and email regularly during training week.

Candidate Journey Overview

A simple step-by-step overview to help you understand what happens first, next, and later.

01 · Pre-Departure

Confirm your apartment and online training details

Confirm your flight, save your branch-prepared housing address, prepare your laptop and phone, and make sure you receive your Training Information Packet before training week.

02 · Arrival Day

Travel directly to your apartment

Land at Incheon, purchase a SIM if needed, then travel directly to the housing prepared by your branch.

03 · Week 1 Training

Attend online training from your apartment

Join training online via Zoom from your apartment and watch closely for emails from the Training Team.

04 · Post-Training

Branch onboarding & setup

Begin in-housing training at your branch. Branch staff will assist with the medical check, ARC, bank account, and onboarding steps.

If anything changes, tell your recruiter, placement manager, and branch staff as early as possible.
Pre-Departure Checklist
Stage 01 · Pre-Departure
  • ✓Send your flight itinerary and receipt to your recruiter and placement manager for reimbursement purposes.
  • ✓Confirm that you receive your branch-prepared housing information before departure for Korea.
  • ✓Prepare a mobile device and laptop. A Windows laptop is recommended when possible.
  • ✓Check with your local bank that your credit and debit cards are authorized for international use.
  • ✓Download Naver Map and Papago Translator.
  • ✓Prepare enough Korean won cash for arrival-period expenses. Recommended arrival cash amount: 1,550,000 KRW.
  • ✓Make sure you receive and understand the Training Information Packet before training week. Check your spam/junk folder if you do not see it.
  • ✓Create your teacher’s profile at teachinkorea.com.
  • ✓Print this guide before leaving your home country and keep it with your travel documents as a backup.
Estimated Arrival Cash Budget
Recommended cash to have available when you arrive in Korea 1,550,000 KRW

Arrival Cash Breakdown

  • Transportation from airport to housing100,000 KRW
  • Early living and setup expenses1,450,000 KRW
Early living and setup estimate includes:
  • Maintenance fee / utilities buffer150,000 KRW*
  • Daily expenses1,000,000 KRW*
  • Miscellaneous setup costs300,000 KRW*

Plan for 4+ weeks until your first pay. The arrival amount should be prepared in cash in KRW. Miscellaneous may include medical exam fee, ARC application fee, housing setup items, and other early setup costs. *Average amount for Seoul; actual costs may vary by location, branch-prepared housing setup, and personal spending.

Useful Apps
Stage 01 · Pre-Departure

Install these apps before departure so you are ready for online training, communication, directions, and translation in Korea.

KakaoTalk · Messenger Primary communication app in Korea. Set up before departure if possible.
Naver Map · Directions Recommended for public transportation and walking routes in Korea.
Papago · Translator Helpful Korean–English translation app for daily life.
Optional transit apps: Kakao Metro and Kakao Bus may also be helpful for subway and bus route details.
Arrival Day at Incheon Airport
Stage 02 · Arrival Day
  • ✓Clear immigration and collect luggage.
  • ✓Purchase a prepaid SIM card at the airport if you need immediate mobile data.
  • ✓Choose transportation and travel directly to your branch-prepared housing.
  • ✓Follow the housing move-in instructions provided by your branch.
  • ✓Notify your recruiter, placement manager, and branch staff if any issue occurs.
Recommended SIM purchase: Once you leave the airport, prepaid SIM cards may be harder to find. Purchasing at Incheon Airport is recommended if you need mobile data immediately.
Important taxi note: Use Orange / White / Silver taxis ONLY. Do not take Black Deluxe taxis. Late-night rides after 10 PM may cost more.
Prepaid SIM Cards: What to Know
Stage 02 · Arrival Day

Recommended if you need mobile data immediately after arriving at Incheon Airport. KT SIM cards are recommended because they are usually the cheapest option and service quality is good.

  • ✓Prepaid SIM cards usually offer unlimited data.
  • ✓You may not be able to make outgoing phone calls or send text messages.
  • ✓You can receive phone calls and text messages.
  • ✓Emergency calls work.
  • ✓Ask at the counter if you need an outgoing-call plan; it may cost more.
Typical 30-day plan pricesNotes
KT & LG U+Usually around 65,000 KRW
SKTAround 71,500 KRW
Prices may change. Pay in KRW only.

Transportation: Taxi to Branch-Prepared Housing
Stage 02 · Arrival Day

Use the Korean address provided by your branch before departure. Save a screenshot in case your mobile data is not working immediately after arrival.

  • ✓Use this if you want the simplest direct ride to your apartment.
  • ✓Expect higher rates after 10 PM.
  • ✓Use regular Orange, White, or Silver taxis only.
  • ✓If you feel unsure, contact your branch or recruiter before leaving the airport.
Airport Bus: Terminal Bus Areas
Stage 02 · Arrival Day

Use the terminal map that matches your arrival terminal. Confirm signage, platform number, destination, and final stop after arrival.

Terminal 1 · Airport Bus Area

Check the platform number and destination before boarding.

Terminal 2 · Airport Bus Area

Confirm terminal, bus stop, and final destination with your branch.

Tip: If the route is unclear, contact your branch before boarding or take a taxi to your apartment.
Airport Bus: Search Your Route
Stage 02 · Arrival Day

Ask your branch for your destination name or nearest bus stop before departure. Use that information to search the airport bus route before boarding.

  • 1Ask your branch: Get the exact destination name or nearest bus stop in advance.
  • 2Search before boarding: Search the route using the airport bus website or airport information screens.
  • 3Confirm the bus: Confirm terminal, platform, destination, and final stop before getting on the bus.
Search the airport bus route before boarding
Airport Bus: Late-Night Arrivals
Stage 02 · Arrival Day

Use late-night bus information only if you arrive late and regular routes are unavailable.

Terminal 1 · Late-Night Bus

Confirm destination and operating hours after arrival.

Terminal 2 · Late-Night Bus

If unsure, contact your branch before boarding.

Late-night note: Late-night bus routes and schedules may change.
Arriving at Your Branch-Arranged Apartment
Stage 02 · Arrival Day

Use the apartment instructions provided by your branch.

  • ✓Go directly to the apartment address provided by your branch.
  • ✓Follow the check-in, key, or entry instructions provided by your branch.
  • ✓Confirm building name, room/unit number, and entry method before traveling.
  • ✓Message your branch contact after arriving safely.
  • ✓Contact your branch immediately if there is an entry, key, or address issue.
Have these readyDetails
Korean addressSave the apartment address in Korean and as a screenshot.
Building / unit informationConfirm the building name, room/unit number, and entry/key instructions.
Branch contactKeep your branch contact available on KakaoTalk or email.
Week 1: Online Training Overview
Stage 03 · Week 1 Training
  • ✓Attend online training via Zoom from your apartment during the first week.
  • ✓Prepare a quiet training space, stable internet connection, laptop charger, notebook, and headset if available.
  • ✓Training Team will send the Training Information Packet the Thursday / Friday KST before training begins.
  • ✓Check the packet carefully for Zoom links, login instructions, training schedule, attendance expectations, and required preparation.
  • ✓You will receive your training result email on Friday.
  • ✓If your branch asks you to attend a branch meeting, housing setup appointment, or onboarding activity during training week, notify your placement manager as soon as possible.
Apartment Internet & online training setup: Your branch will help with Internet setup or troubleshooting if support is needed.
  • ✓Set up your laptop, charger, and webcam in a quiet area of your apartment.
  • ✓Confirm your Internet connection as soon as possible after arrival.
  • ✓Install or update Zoom before training day.
  • ✓Check your microphone, camera, and speaker settings.
  • ✓Keep your phone available for KakaoTalk/email communication.
Laptop reminder: A Windows laptop is recommended when possible for training compatibility.
Online TrainingDetails
Training method Online via Zoom from your branch-prepared housing
Training Team training@creverse-fc.com
02-6420-8121 / 8122
02-2219-7459 / 7490 / 9409
Training packet reminder: Make sure you receive and understand the Training Information Packet before training week. Check your spam/junk folder if you do not see it.
Post-Training: Branch Onboarding & Setup
Stage 04 · Post-Training

After online training, you may have in-housing training and branch onboarding at your assigned branch.

StagePost-training instructions
In-housing training at branch Attend branch-level training, onboarding, shadowing, or setup activities as instructed by branch staff.
Medical check Branch staff will assist you with the required medical check process after training.
Alien Registration Card (ARC) Branch staff will assist with the ARC application process and related instructions.
Bank account Branch staff will assist with opening a Korean bank account when the required documents are ready.
Prepare before arrival: Confirm your apartment address, move-in instructions, branch directions, onboarding schedule, daily expenses, medical exam fee, ARC application fee, and any documents your branch asks you to bring.
Welcome to Korea!

Pre-Departure
Confirm apartment, flight, Zoom, apps, laptop, and cash.

Before You Leave Home

  • Send flight itinerary and receipt.
  • Prepare laptop and mobile device.
  • Set up KakaoTalk.
  • Download Naver Map and Papago.
  • Prepare 1,550,000 KRW arrival cash.
  • Receive and understand the Training Information Packet.
  • Create teacher profile.

Arrival Day Order

  • Clear immigration and collect luggage.
  • Buy prepaid SIM if needed.
  • Save apartment address in Korean.
  • Choose taxi, airport bus, or local transit based on your branch-provided address.
  • Travel directly to your branch-prepared housing.
  • Confirm building name, unit number, and entry method.
  • Notify staff if an issue occurs.
Taxi / Housing Quick Card
Branch-prepared housing · Use the apartment address provided before departure
Orange / White / Silver taxis only No Black Deluxe taxis Confirm move-in instructions Save Korean address screenshot Contact branch if unsure Airport bus only if route is clear

Online Training

Online via Zoom from your apartment. Set up your laptop, charger, webcam, microphone, Zoom, and Internet connection before training begins.

training@creverse-fc.com
02-6420-8121 / 8122

Quick Contacts

Tim Lee: timlee@creverse.com · 02-6420-8072
Fiorella Im: fiorellaim@creverse.com · 02-6420-7000

Emergency: 112 · Foreigner Hotline: 1330

Online Training + Post-Training Checklist
1Set up your training space in your apartment before training begins.
2Join Zoom on time using the link provided by the Training Team.
3Keep your camera, audio, charger, and materials ready during training.
4Check email and KakaoTalk regularly for Training Team updates.
5Complete training assignments and preparation as instructed.
6Watch for your training result email on Friday.

Post-Training

Branch onboarding: After online training, begin in-housing training and onboarding at your assigned branch. Apartment information will be provided before departure for Korea.

  • Confirm branch onboarding schedule
  • Save branch and apartment addresses
  • Attend in-housing training
  • Complete onboarding steps
  • Prepare daily expenses
  • Prepare medical exam, ARC, and bank setup fees

Who to Contact When...

Housing move-in issuePlacement Manager
Flight / receipt / arrival questionRecruiter
Training schedule / Zoom linkTraining Center / Training Team
Branch directions / housing / onboarding / ARC / bankBranch Staff
Emergency112 / 1330

Final Arrival Checklist

Send required receipts and itinerary. Set up KakaoTalk before departure. Print this guide before departure as a travel backup. Save apartment, branch, and online training information. Prepare KRW cash for transportation and early living expenses. Do not change housing or arrival plans without approval. Contact recruiter and placement manager early if anything changes.
Welcome to Korea!
